name: Profile_R5_ResearchStudy_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.ResearchStudy resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 ResearchStudy resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 ResearchStudy resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.ResearchStudy resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 ResearchStudy resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 ResearchStudy resources.

element.short: R5: Canonical identifier for this study resource (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.url` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.url` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The business version for the study record (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.version` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.version` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:

Element `ResearchStudy.name` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `ResearchStudy.descriptionSummary` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.classifier` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.

element.short: R5: Sponsors, collaborators, and other parties (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.associatedParty` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.associatedParty` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
For a Sponsor or a PrincipalInvestigator use the dedicated attributes provided.

element.comment:

Element `ResearchStudy.progressStatus` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Estimated total number of participants to be enrolled (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.targetNumber` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.targetNumber` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Actual total number of participants enrolled in study (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.actualNumber` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.actualNumber` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Group of participants who were enrolled in study (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.actualGroup` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.actualGroup` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: A variable measured during the study (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.outcomeMeasure` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.outcomeMeasure` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
A study may have multiple distinct outcome measures that can be used to assess the overall goal for a study. The goal of a study is in the objective whereas the metric by which the goal is assessed is the outcomeMeasure. Examples: Time to Local Recurrence (TLR), Disease-free Survival (DFS), 30 Day Mortality, Systolic BP

element.short: R5: Link to results generated during the study (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has a context of ResearchStudy based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.result` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: draft | active | retired | unknown additional codes
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.status` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ResearchStudy.status` as `RelatedTo`.
The mappings for `ResearchStudy.status` do not allow expression of the necessary codes, per the bindings on the source and target.
The target context `ResearchStudy.status` is a modifier element, so this extension does not need to be defined as a modifier.

element.short: R5: focus additional types
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.focus` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ResearchStudy.focus` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `ResearchStudy.focus` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.

element.short: R5: Inclusion and exclusion criteria additional types
element.definition: R5: `ResearchStudy.recruitment.eligibility` additional types from child elements (display, identifier, reference, type)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.eligibility` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ResearchStudy.enrollment` as `RelatedTo`.
The mappings for `ResearchStudy.recruitment.eligibility` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: display, identifier, reference, type.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `ResearchStudy.recruitment.eligibility` with unmapped reference targets: EvidenceVariable.

element.short: R5: site
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.site` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ResearchStudy.site` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `ResearchStudy.site` with unmapped reference targets: Organization, ResearchStudy.

element.short: R5: Allows the comparisonGroup for the study and the comparisonGroup for the subject to be linked easily (new)
element.comment:
Element `ResearchStudy.comparisonGroup.linkId` has a context of ResearchStudy.arm based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ResearchStudy`.
Element `ResearchStudy.comparisonGroup.linkId` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
